content:
# Acid Chemistry

**Summary**: The chemical behavior of acid mixtures in reservoir environments.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2012-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2012]] analyzed the dissociation and chelation of organic acids.

- [[2015-barri|Barri, 2015]] investigated the chemistry of various chelating agents as alternatives to HCl.

## Scientific Context

- [[2012-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2012]] The chemical behavior of acid mixtures in reservoir environments.

- [[2015-barri|Barri, 2015]] investigated the chemistry of various chelating agents as alternatives to HCl.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2012-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2012]] Chemical equilibrium calculations

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Organic acids are weak and their dissociation is slow, leading to retarded HF release. ([[2012-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2012, p. 36]])
- [[2012-al-harbi|Al-Harbi, 2012]] pKa values: Citric (3.128), Formic (3.75), Acetic (4.756)

- EDTA was not soluble at pH values less than 5.0 when sea water was used for dilution. ([[2015-barri|Barri, 2015, p. 22]])
